title: 10 best herbal teas for hemorrhoids
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hemorrhoids are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids, such as inflammation, pain, and itching. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce swelling and provide relief. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for hemorrhoids include chamomile, witch hazel, and calendula. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, while witch hazel and calendula have astringent and healing effects. Drinking these teas can improve the lives of individuals suffering from hemorrhoids by reducing their discomfort and promoting healing, allowing them to engage in daily activities without pain or irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut teas helps with hemorrhoids because it contains aescin, a bioflavonoid that strengthens and tones blood vessels, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemroids. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the affected area, providing relief from itching, burning, and pain. Additionally, horse chestnut's vasoconstrictive properties can help reduce swelling and bleeding, allowing for a quicker healing process.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a saponin found in horse chestnut, helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in hemorrhoidal tissues, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the affected area, reducing bleeding and itching associated with hemorrhoids.
Tannins: Tannins in horse chestnut tea help to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce bleeding and alleviate discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Bark: Contains astringent tannins that can help constrict bleeding and shrink swollen tissues, providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horse chestnut fruits from mid-summer to autumn.
Clean and sort the horse chestnut fruits removing any impurities or debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried horse chestnut for every cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried horse chestnut in boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes then strain.
Consume the tea hot or chilled within an hour of preparation and discard any leftovers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick teas helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and reduce swelling in the rectal area. The tea's flavonoids and anthocyanins also help to improve blood flow and strengthen blood vessel walls, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, kinnikinnick's astringent properties can help to shrink swollen tissues and provide relief from itching and irritation.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The tannins in Kinnikinnick tea help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial compounds can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to hemorrhoid development and exacerbate symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Kinnikinnick tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain, itching, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and other compounds, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of starches, rhyzomes can help absorb excess fluid and ease discomfort caused by hemorrhoidal flare-ups.
Bark: The bark's astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, making it an effective ingredient in teas for hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried or fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves in early summer.
Boil one cup of pure water in a tea kettle for five minutes before steeping the kinnikinnick leaves.
Add the harvested leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a teapot using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth after the steeping process is complete.
Serve the herbal tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and drink within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with hemorrhoids because it is rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and soothing properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The flavonoids present in white tea, such as catechins, have been shown to improve blood circulation and ease constipation, both of which are common contributors to hemorrhoid issues. Additionally, the calming effects of white tea can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, further aiding in the relief from hemorrhoidal discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids by protecting against oxidative stress and DNA damage.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help soothe and calm irritated tissues in the rectal area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Astringent properties: Some herbal white teas, like witch hazel or oak bark tea, have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding associated with hemorrhoids, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Bark: Contains tannins and phenolic acids that act as astringents, reducing swelling and bleeding in hemorrhoidal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of loose-leaf white tea leaves for every eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 150°f and 160°f in a kettle or pot.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves in a teapot or infuser and let steep for 2-3 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or infuser and discard the tea leaves.
Add honey or other desired sweeteners to taste and serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stoneroot teas helps with hemorrhoids because it contains a compound called sanguinarine, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When consumed as a tea, stoneroot helps to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with hemorrhoids, promoting healing and relief from discomfort. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects can help to calm irritation and inflammation in the anal area, providing welcome respite from the discomfort of hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Stoneroot tea have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in Stoneroot tea can help to shrink swollen hemorrhoidal tissue and promote blood clotting, reducing bleeding and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Stoneroot tea, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help soothe and shrink swollen hemorrhoidal tissue.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that can reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing of hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried collinsonia canadensis leaves in the morning when dew drops still cling to them gently.
Rinse one teaspoon of dried stoneroot leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves with filtered water carefully.
Boil one cup of filtered water and pour it over the herbal mixture allowing 5-7 minutes steeping time patiently.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container slowly.
Drink the stoneroot tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 days in advance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with hemorrhoids because of its rich source of silica, a mineral that strengthens and tonesthe connective tissue in the anal canal. This can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, horsetail contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can also help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Horsetail tea helps reduce swelling, bleeding, and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and drying up excess moisture.

Antioxidant properties: Horsetail tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin and mucous membranes in the rectal area from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of further irritation and discomfort.

Vitamin C and silica content: Horsetail tea is rich in vitamin C and silica, which can help strengthen blood vessels, improve collagen production, and support overall digestive health, all of which can contribute to improved hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silicon and other minerals, horsetail roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail stems equisetum arvense in large quantities for tea preparation.
Cut the harvested horsetail into smaller pieces and rinse them with cold water to remove dirt.
Combine one teaspoon of dried horsetail with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the tea reaches your desired strength and flavor.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then let the tea cool to room temperature for serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet teas helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated tissues surrounding the anal canal. The tea's natural astringent compounds also help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief from discomfort, itching, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, meadowsweet's gentle antimicrobial effects may help to prevent infection and promote healing of affected areas.
remedy_properties: Salicylic acid: Meadowsweet contains salicylic acid, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Tannins: The tannins present in meadowsweet tea have astringent properties that help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.

Flavonoids: Meadowsweet's flavonoid compounds, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the anal area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Meadowsweet roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, providing relief from hemorrhoidal discomfort.
Bark: The bark of the meadowsweet plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including hemorrhoids, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried meadowsweet flowers at dawn on a dry day weighing about 10 grams.
Measure accurately 10 grams of dried meadowsweet flowers into a heat-resistant glass infuser or tea ball.
Bring fresh filtered water to boil in a medium-sized pot and pour it over the herbal mixture.
Allow the herbal tea to steep for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor profile.
Strain the tea immediately after steeping and discard the solids then serve hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alder buckthorn teas helps with hemorrhoids because it contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have a gentle, yet effective laxative effect that can help to soften and ease bowel movements. This can reduce the pressure and strain on the rectal veins, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting healing and reducing swelling.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds have a laxative effect, which helps to soften and ease bowel movements, reducing pressure on hemorrhoidal veins and providing relief from symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in alder buckthorn tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the rectal area, making it more comfortable for individuals with hemorrhoids.
Tannins: Tannins in alder buckthorn tea have astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and anthraquinones, which help soothe and reduce inflammation in the rectal area.
Bark: Contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried frangula alnus bark or root and rinse it gently with cold water.
Combine the rinsed herbs with 4 cups of boiling water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes, allowing the tea to infuse slowly.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass container.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating it at 39 degrees fahrenheit within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with hemorrhoids because it contains tannins, astringent compounds that constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties of witch hazel also soothe the affected area, reducing discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, the tea's ability to improve circulation can help reduce pressure on the veins in the rectal area, further alleviating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch hazel's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation, itching, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and drying up excess fluid.
Antiseptic properties: Witch hazel's antiseptic properties prevent infection and promote healing by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch hazel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids by reducing the production of prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are known to contribute to inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Witch hazel roots are rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and shrink swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains a high concentration of gallic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that aid in soothing and shrinking hemorrhoidal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves from late spring to early summer for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested witch hazel leaves thoroughly with clean towels or air circulation.
Combine one teaspoon of dried witch hazel leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties slowly.
Strain and discard the solids, then serve the tea hot or chilled as desired, sweetening with honey if preferred.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoid symptoms. The tea also contains flavonoids that help to improve blood vessel tone, reducing pressure on the anal veins and preventing further irritation. Additionally, St John's wort has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, providing relief from itching, burning, and pain caused by hemorrhoids. Drinking this tea regularly can provide long-term relief from hemorrhoid symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce bleeding from hemorrhoids.
Hypericin: This compound has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent infections that may develop as a result of hemorrhoidal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation to alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Flowers: Abundant in anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin around the anus.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried st john's wort flowers and leaves for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Combine the st john's wort herb with the boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to release into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or a piece of cheesecloth to separate the solids from the liquid.
Enjoy your herbal tea hot or chilled within an hour of preparation, as its potency may decrease over time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with hemorrhoids because its soothing and calming properties can reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help to relax the anal sphincter muscles and reduce swelling around the anus. This relief can provide instant comfort for individuals experiencing hemorrhoid symptoms, allowing them to find temporary reprieve from painful episodes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected area, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender's flavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on the mucous membranes, reducing swelling and itching, while also alleviating pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Lavender's essential oils have been shown to accelerate wound healing and prevent infection, which can help speed up the recovery process for individuals with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and reduce swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Bark: Contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried lavender buds per cup of boiling water.
Heat 250ml of water in a pot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil.
Pour the boiling water over the measured lavender buds in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving hot.
Sweeten with honey to taste if desired, and serve immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hemorrhoids with herbal teas is by drinking tea made from herbs such as witch hazel, chamomile, and calendula. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the affected area. Additionally, drinking tea made from butcher's broom can help improve blood flow and strengthen vein walls, which can alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids. It is important to drink at least 2-3 cups of these herbal teas daily for best results. However, it is always recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal teas include anal fissures and inflammatory bowel disease. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and heal damaged tissues in the anal area. Meanwhile, teas made from herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root can help treat inflammatory bowel disease by coating the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Peppermint tea is also commonly used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, which can sometimes be mistaken for hemorrhoids. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas to treat any medical condition.
